description_long: This is a roughly oval, disk-shaped stone featuring a detailed bas-relief carving of a crab on its front face. The crab is depicted with prominent claws, segmented legs, and a textured carapace, all raised from the stone's surface, giving it a three-dimensional appearance. The stone's edge is thick and rounded, with a slightly rough, weathered texture, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements. The back of the stone is relatively flat and unadorned, while the sides reveal the depth of the carving and the thickness of the stone. The coloration is primarily a mix of gray and brown tones, with subtle variations that enhance the naturalistic look of both the stone and the crab. This object could serve as a decorative or commemorative feature in an urban park, plaza, or garden.
description: An oval stone with a detailed bas-relief carving of a crab, featuring a thick, rounded edge and natural gray-brown coloration.
description_view_0: Side view showing the stone's thickness and the crab's legs extending from the relief.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the full crab carving with detailed claws and carapace.
description_view_2: Opposite side view emphasizing the depth of the relief and the stone's rounded edge.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the flat, uncarved back of the stone with only the crab's outline visible.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground or mounted on a low pedestal
attribute_pattern: Bas-relief carving of a crab with naturalistic texturing and subtle color variations; no regular geometric pattern.
attribute_weathering: Moderately rough and weathered, with some surface irregularities suggesting exposure to outdoor conditions.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or landscaped urban environments, serving as a focal decorative element; may be partially surrounded by plants or low barriers.
